buildings.name: NEK Krško Nuclear Power Plant
buildings.info: Slovenia's only nuclear plant (696 MW), on the Sava. Jointly owned with Croatia; supplies ~20% of national electricity.

buildings.name: TEŠ Šoštanj Thermal Power Plant
buildings.info: Lignite-fired plant in the Šaleška valley. Its 600 MW Unit 6 is Slovenia's single largest power source.

buildings.name: HE Zlatoličje
buildings.info: Run-of-river hydro plant on the Drava (~136 MW), fed by a long headrace canal.

buildings.name: HE Formin
buildings.info: Hydroelectric plant on the Drava (~116 MW), the last of the Drava chain before Croatia.

buildings.name: HE Dravograd
buildings.info: Oldest of the Drava hydro plants (1943), ~26 MW, at the Austrian border.

buildings.name: HE Moste
buildings.info: Slovenia's oldest large hydro plant (1952) on the Sava near Žirovnica, ~21 MW.

buildings.name: HE Krško
buildings.info: Run-of-river hydro plant on the lower Sava (~39 MW), part of the Sava HE chain.

buildings.name: Avče Pumped-Storage Plant
buildings.info: Slovenia's only pumped-storage hydro plant (185 MW) on the Soča/Idrijca, for grid balancing.

buildings.name: Ljubljana Castle
buildings.info: Medieval hilltop castle above Ljubljana, first documented in 1144; now a museum and event venue.

buildings.name: Predjama Castle
buildings.info: Renaissance castle built into a cliff-face cave mouth near Postojna — the largest cave castle in the world.

buildings.name: Bled Castle
buildings.info: Cliff-top castle overlooking Lake Bled, first mentioned in 1011 — the oldest Slovenian castle.

buildings.name: Ptuj Castle
buildings.info: Hilltop castle above Ptuj, Slovenia's oldest town; houses a regional museum.

buildings.name: Ljubljana Cathedral (St. Nicholas)
buildings.info: Baroque cathedral with a green dome and twin towers, completed 1706.

buildings.name: Maribor Cathedral
buildings.info: Gothic Cathedral of St. John the Baptist, seat of the Archdiocese of Maribor.

buildings.name: Church on Bled Island
buildings.info: Pilgrimage Church of the Assumption on the island in Lake Bled, famous for its wishing bell.

buildings.name: Slovenian National Assembly
buildings.info: Seat of Slovenia's parliament in Ljubljana, noted for its sculpted 1959 portal.

buildings.name: Presidential Palace
buildings.info: Office of the President of Slovenia on Erželjčeva ulica, Ljubljana.

buildings.name: Ljubljana Town Hall
buildings.info: Seat of the City of Ljubljana, next to Robba's Fountain on Mestni trg.

buildings.name: Nebotičnik
buildings.info: 1933 Art Deco skyscraper (70 m); briefly the tallest residential building in Europe.

buildings.name: Crystal Palace (Kristalna palača)
buildings.info: 89 m office tower in BTC City, Ljubljana — the tallest building in Slovenia.

buildings.name: Cankarjev dom
buildings.info: Slovenia's largest cultural and congress centre, in Ljubljana.

buildings.name: SNG Opera and Ballet Ljubljana
buildings.info: Neo-Renaissance national opera house, opened 1892.

buildings.name: National & University Library (NUK)
buildings.info: Jože Plečnik's 1941 masterpiece and Slovenia's national library.

buildings.name: Stožice Stadium
buildings.info: 16,000-seat national football stadium in Ljubljana, opened 2010.

buildings.name: Arena Stožice
buildings.info: 12,500-seat indoor arena beside Stožice Stadium, under a shared shell.

buildings.name: Ljudski vrt
buildings.info: Maribor's football stadium, home of NK Maribor.

buildings.name: Planica Nordic Centre
buildings.info: Ski-jumping and Nordic centre hosting the world's largest ski-flying hill.

buildings.name: Ljubljana Railway Station
buildings.info: Main railway station of Ljubljana, opened 1849 on the Vienna–Trieste line.

buildings.name: Ljubljana Jože Pučnik Airport
buildings.info: Slovenia's main international airport, at Brnik near Kranj.

buildings.name: Revoz (Renault) Plant
buildings.info: Renault's car factory in Novo mesto — Slovenia's only volume car plant and top exporter.

buildings.name: Krka Pharmaceuticals
buildings.info: Headquarters and plants of Krka, one of Slovenia's largest generic-drug makers, Novo mesto.

buildings.name: Gorenje Factory
buildings.info: Home-appliance manufacturer in Velenje, a major Slovenian industrial employer.

buildings.name: Lek / Sandoz
buildings.info: Pharmaceutical maker in Ljubljana, part of the Sandoz/Novartis group.

buildings.name: Cinkarna Celje
buildings.info: Chemical and titanium-dioxide producer in Celje, one of Slovenia's oldest industrial firms.
